title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's Church of the Open Word will be converted into six condos and a new five-story building with 26 units.
The 1893 church, initially considered for an arts center, was bought by Habitech Communities for $4 million in 2024.
Greater Boston sees a trend of converting churches to residences as seen with projects in Jamaica Plain and Bay Village.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Celtics could target Kawhi Leonard, a potential trade candidate, for his versatility and championship experience.
LeBron James, a top unrestricted free agent, may fit Boston's lineup needs despite his long-shot status.
John Collins offers forward depth; Coby White and Jaxson Hayes provide potential guard and center options.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time after defeating Molloy University.
Endicott College secured its fourth consecutive NCAA Division III championship appearance by beating Salve Regina University.
Bentley's Stan DeMartinis III scored the game-winning run, while Endicott's offense included home runs by AJ Hamm and TJ Liponis.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn forward Alex Karaban next week ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban ranks No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 draft prospects; Celtics have the No. 27 and No. 40 picks.
Karaban, a Southborough native, scored 1,880 points and won 126 games during his four years at UConn.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at Boston's MGM Music Hall, mixing jazz and pop from his new album and older hits.
Puth's 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour' spans 22 countries, emphasizing musicianship with stripped-down aesthetics and jazz influences.
Opening acts Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey warmed up the audience with indie-pop sounds and live looping performances.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot, celebrating its 30th anniversary.
Jakob Dylan and the Wallflowers followed their classic album with Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' in concert.
The setlist included hits like 'One Headlight' and covers of Petty's 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting' in the encore.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston's population declined by 1,338 people, marking the largest loss in Massachusetts last year.
Everett gained the most residents, adding 2,362 people, followed by Worcester with an increase of 1,916.
Massachusetts' overall population grew by 15,500, a 0.2% increase, amid slower growth and shifts in migration.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Market Basket's parent company ranked in top family businesses in Forbes list.
Fidelity Investments, owned by the Johnson family, ranked 14th with $37.7 billion in revenue.
Demoulas Super Markets, fully owned by the Demoulas family, ranked 69th earning $8 billion annually.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a comprehensive campus housing revitalization project.
The project aims to modernize residential life, maintain affordability, and expand student amenities at the flagship campus.
Redevelopment will occur in phases, accommodating housing needs amidst rising enrollment and regional housing shortages.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre in Medford, showcasing songs from her new album, 'World's Gone Wrong'.
Williams' setlist included protest songs, covers, and classics like 'Car Wheels on a Gravel Road' and 'Joy'.
The concert featured themes of political dissent, with Williams addressing societal issues and using music as a form of protest.
